concepts.title: R2P (Responsibility to Protect)
concepts.description: R2P is a doctrine that emphasizes the international community's responsibility to protect civilians from mass atrocities, such as genocide, war crimes, and ethnic cleansing.

concepts.title: Global Governance
concepts.description: The concept of global governance refers to the rules and norms that govern international relations, but it does not imply the existence of a single, centralized authority.

concepts.title: ASEAN Way
concepts.description: The ASEAN Way is a set of principles and practices that guide the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) in promoting regional cooperation and stability, emphasizing consensus-based decision-making and non-interference in internal affairs.

concepts.title: P5 in the UN Security Council
concepts.description: Mnemonics: 'P5 stands for Powerful 5' - USA, UK, France, Russia, China.

concepts.title: Confusing Soft Power with Hard Power
concepts.description: Soft power refers to the ability of a state to achieve its goals through attraction, persuasion, and influence, whereas hard power is based on coercion, military force, or economic sanctions. Students often confuse these two concepts, so it's essential to understand the difference.

concepts.title: Key Features of Global Governance
concepts.description: Units to remember: Fragmentation, Interdependence, Globalization, Informal Governance, and International Law.

concepts.title: UN Charter
concepts.description: The UN Charter sets out the purposes and principles of the United Nations, including the promotion of peace, security, and cooperation among nations, as well as the protection of human rights and fundamental freedoms.

concepts.title: Human Security
concepts.description: Human security emphasizes the protection of individuals and communities from threats such as poverty, hunger, disease, and conflict, rather than solely focusing on state security.
